params.name: active
params.description: Only return prices that are active or inactive (e.g., pass `false` to list all inactive prices).

params.name: created
params.description: A filter on the list, based on the object `created` field. The value can be a string with an integer Unix timestamp, or it can be a dictionary with a number of different query options.

params.name: currency
params.description: Only return prices for the given currency.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: lookup_keys
params.description: Only return the price with these lookup_keys, if any exist.

params.name: product
params.description: Only return prices for the given product.

params.name: recurring
params.description: Only return prices with these recurring fields.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

params.name: type
params.description: Only return prices of type `recurring` or `one_time`.

items.docs: Returns a list of your active prices, excluding inline prices. For the list of inactive prices, set active to false.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: page
params.description: A cursor for pagination across multiple pages of results. Don't include this parameter on the first call. Use the next_page value returned in a previous response to request subsequent results.

params.name: query
params.description: The search query string. See [search query language](https://stripe.com/docs/search#search-query-language) and the list of supported [query fields for prices](https://stripe.com/docs/search#query-fields-for-prices).

items.docs: Search for prices you’ve previously created using Stripe’s Search Query Language. Don’t use search in read-after-write flows where strict consistency is necessary. Under normal operating conditions, data is searchable in less than a minute. Occasionally, propagation of new or updated data can be up to an hour behind during outages. Search functionality is not available to merchants in India.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the price with the given ID.
Creates a new price for an existing product. The price can be recurring or one-time.
Updates the specified price by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided are left unchanged.
